review:
✔️ 300 Saatin Ardından: Bu Oyun Tam Bir Bağımlılık ve Sinir Harbi!

Oyuna sıfırdan, geçmişte hiç CS:GO falan oynamadan doğrudan CS2 ile girdim ve an itibarıyla 300 saati devirmiş bulunmaktayım. Dışarıdan bakınca "altı üstü bomba kurup çözüyorsun" denilen bu oyunun insanı nasıl içine çektiğini şimdi çok iyi anlıyorum.

Eğer benim gibi türe çok eski olmayan birinin gözünden bu oyunun ne olduğunu merak ediyorsanız, durum tam olarak şu:

✔️ Neden 300 Saattir Oynuyorum? (İyi Yanları)

* Vuruş Hissiyatı ve Mekanikler: Silahların geri tepmesini (spray) öğrenmek, o merminin tam kafaya gittiği andaki (headshot) ses ve tatmin hissi gerçekten muazzam. Başka hiçbir FPS oyununda bu vuruş hissiyatı yok.

* Grafikler ve Sis Mekaniği: Oyunun görselliği, ışıklandırmaları gayet modern ve temiz duruyor. Özellikle sis bombalarının (smoke) içine el bombası atıp geçici kör noktalar yaratma olayı taktiksel olarak oyuna çok büyük keyif katıyor.

* Premier Hırsı: Oyundaki en büyük motivasyon kaynağım kesinlikle Premier modundaki o puanı (CS Rating) yükseltme hırsı. O puanı bir tık daha yukarı taşımak, rütbe atlamak için her gün kendinizi paralıyorsunuz, tam bir bağımlılık döngüsü.

* Skin Dünyası: Pazarda saatlerce gezip bütçene göre en havalı skini seçmek, oyunda o silahı eline alıp incelemek (F tuşuna basıp durmak) ayrı bir zevk. Envanteri güzelleştirmek oyuna çok ayrı bir tat katıyor.

✔️ 300 Saatte Saçımı Başımı Yolduran Şeyler (Kötü Yanları)

* Hileci Sorunu: Maalesef oyunun en büyük kanseri bu. Seçkin (Prime) hesap almama rağmen, özellikle Premier modunda bazen öyle tipler geliyor ki oyun zevkinin içine ediyorlar. Valve'ın bu hile sistemini acilen çözmesi lazım.

* Mermi Kayıt Sorunları (Sub-tick): Bazen adamın tam üstüne sıktığıma, hatta kan çıktığına yemin edebilirim ama oyun "0 hasar verdin" diyebiliyor. Duvardan mermi yiyip ölme olayları da insanı çileden çıkarıyor.

* Toksik Kitle: Ruslar başta olmak üzere, mikrofondan sürekli bağıran, infoyu yanlış veren veya hiç oynamayıp oyunu sabote eden tipler moral bozabiliyor.

✔️ Sonuç:
CS2 tam bir "ne seninle, ne sensiz" oyunu. Söve söve kapatıyorsun, sinirlenip siliyorsun ama akşam olunca elin yine o "Oyna" butonuna gidiyor. O Premier puanını yükseltme hırsı ve envantere yeni skin ekleme isteği insanı bir şekilde ekrana bağlıyor. Rekabet hissini iliklerinize kadar yaşamak istiyorsanız kesinlikle şans verin, ama sinirlerinize de hakim olun.

✔️ Puanım: 7 / 10 ✔️

review_english: After 300 Hours, This Game Is A Total Addiction And A Nerve Wreck! I started the game from scratch, directly with CS2, without having ever played CS GO in the past, and I have completed 300 hours as of now. From the outside, I now understand very well how this game, which is called "you set up and defuse bombs", draws people in. If you are wondering what this game is from the perspective of someone like me who is not very old in the genre, this is exactly the situation: Why Have I Been Playing For 300 Hours? (Good Points) Hit Feeling and Mechanics Learning the recoil of guns (spray), the sound and feeling of satisfaction when that bullet hits the head (headshot) is truly amazing. No other FPS game has this shooting feeling. Graphics and Fog Mechanics The visuals and lighting of the game look very modern and clean. Especially throwing grenades into smoke bombs and creating temporary blind spots adds great tactical pleasure to the game. Premier Ambition My biggest source of motivation in the game is definitely the ambition to increase that score (CS Rating) in the Premier mode. You're beating yourself up every day to move that score one step higher, to move up the ranks, it's a complete cycle of addiction. Skin World It's a different pleasure to spend hours wandering around the market, choosing the coolest skin according to your budget, holding that weapon in your hand and examining it in the game (holding down the F key). Beautifying the inventory adds a very different flavor to the game. Things that made me tear my hair out in 300 hours (Bad Sides) Cheater Problem Unfortunately, this is the biggest cancer of the game. Even though I have a Prime account, sometimes people come and ruin the fun of the game, especially in the Premier mode. Valve needs to solve this cheat system urgently. Bullet Registration Problems (Sub-tick) Sometimes I could swear that I shot the man right on him and even blood came out, but the game says "You did 0 damage". Incidents of people getting hit by a bullet from a wall and dying are also infuriating. Toxic Crowd People, especially the Russians, who constantly shout from the microphone, give incorrect information, or sabotage the game by not playing at all, can be demoralizing. Conclusion CS2 is a complete "neither with you nor without you" game. You close it after a while, you get angry and delete it, but when evening comes, your hand goes to that "Play" button again. The ambition to increase that Premier score and the desire to add new skins to the inventory somehow binds people to the screen. If you want to experience the feeling of competition in your bones, definitely give it a chance, but control your nerves. My score is 7 out of 10

After a long time in this "Wonderful" game, it's time to leave my feedback on your impressions of it. After playing it for more than 8000 hours, I realized that the skill in it does not affect the gameplay. And that's where to start... Let's start with probably the most important thing in my opinion is the honesty of the gameplay itself, namely the complete absence of anti-cheat, at any moment you can get into a team or against an unfair player. Who will play openly with cheats, and you will not influence it in any way. Complaints are not considered at all. Thus, we have gradually approached the second aspect of the game - the complaint system. Any player who, in his opinion, encounters unfair play can safely leave a complaint against the opponent, thereby lowering his confidence factor. Whether they are justified or not, no one cares. You will just slide lower and lower in terms of the factor, thereby you will start to drain your rank lower and lower, and receive even more complaints because your skill will not match the rank. A vicious circle of constant humiliation and unfair play. Cheaters with expensive weapon skins have been observed more than once. So we move on to the topic of skins.... The skins in the game are pure monopoly and casino roulette. Terminals have been added to explain the importance of the casino in the game. in the terminal, skins are often more expensive than the prices on the trading floor. It's not possible to knock anything out of the container until you spend as much money as the skin will cost. Let's return to the interesting point of monopoly, of course, it is not possible to prove it, valve has already taken care of this. Fighting cheaters is not beneficial to them, the cheater relaxes, destroys your desire to play this "wonderful" game, destroys your rating, destroys your psyche and all your desires regarding the game. They start buying skins, they let their friends in the team, or maybe they're just their second, third, etc. they collect drop cases, cash in on the game, and only at the moment when the cheater becomes displeased, they ban him, the skins that he had burn out, the prices of the skins start to rise, and that's the moment when the corruption starts, then the cheater starts a new account, gives money to valve by buying a new premium account, here's another penny in a piggy bank....
PS
The game and its creators don't care about you and your mental health and condition, they don't care about cheaters, it's just a business, and ordinary players are just a biomusor for them. You yourself accept the agreement where they show you that all the problems with cheaters and other problems are on us, but they even deprived us of a patrol, so that we could not do anything about it. They do not consider complaints, having automated everything.
PPS
Decide for yourself. We are not the ones in this game, just animate bots who are trying to beat a system that has long been rotten from the inside. Thank you all for your attention.

Counter-Strike 2 is actually a pretty fun game… when you’re not getting headshotted through three walls by a guy named “TotallyLegitPlayer”.

The gunplay feels smooth, the graphics look great, and every match has that perfect mix of strategy and chaos. One second you’re pulling off a tactical masterpiece, the next your teammate is flashbanging the entire squad including himself.

Sadly, the biggest boss in the game isn’t the enemy team. Some players have better wall vision than my Wi-Fi router. I swear one guy knew my location before I even loaded into the map.

CS2 can be incredibly addictive and hilarious with friends. Nothing builds friendships faster than screaming “HE’S ONE HP!” while spectating your teammate miss an entire magazine.

Overall:
Great game.
Great mechanics.
Great memes.
Questionable humanity.

8/10 — would get destroyed by suspiciously talented Russians again.

review:
Counter-Strike 2 is still one of the most fun and competitive FPS games ever made. The gunplay is great, the maps are iconic, and there really isn't another game that scratches the same itch. When you get a good match, it's easy to see why people have been playing Counter-Strike for over 20 years.

The biggest problem is the cheating situation. VAC feels like it only works when someone is blatantly rage hacking and making it obvious. Meanwhile, there are plenty of players, including Prime accounts, who try to hide their cheats and make them look legitimate. Anyone who has spent enough time in Premier or Competitive knows exactly what I'm talking about.

What's frustrating is that Valve is a billion-dollar company with more than enough resources to improve anti-cheat, yet it often feels like the issue isn't a priority. Legitimate players are left questioning whether someone is just having a good game or quietly using assistance that never gets detected.

CS2 is still a great game at its core, but it's sad to see it in its current state. The potential is there, the gameplay is there, but the cheating problem continues to hold it back.

review:
CS sempre foi aquele tipo de jogo que atravessa gerações.
Mesmo com todas as mudanças até chegar no CS2, ainda tem aquela essência clássica que faz qualquer partida casual virar competição séria em 2 minutos KKK. O gunplay continua muito satisfatório, as partidas rendem momentos absurdos e dá pra entender fácil porque a franquia virou praticamente um marco dos FPS competitivos.

Mas sendo sincero, atualmente o jogo sofre bastante com cheaters, principalmente quando você começa a tentar jogar de forma mais competitiva. Em alguns momentos isso realmente desanima. Além disso, modos mais sérios como o Premier acabam praticamente exigindo o Prime pra ter uma experiência minimamente decente.

Ainda assim, quando o matchmaking coopera e cai uma partida boa, CS2 continua sendo viciante demais. É aquele jogo que você entra pra jogar ‘só uma’ e quando percebe já perdeu a noção do tempo.

review_english: CS has always been that type of game that spans generations. Even with all the changes until CS2, it still has that classic essence that turns any casual game into a serious competition in 2 minutes LOL. The gunplay remains very satisfying, the matches yield absurd moments and it's easy to understand why the franchise has practically become a landmark in competitive FPS. But to be honest, the game currently suffers a lot from cheaters, especially when you start trying to play more competitively. At times this is really discouraging. Furthermore, more serious modes like Premier end up practically requiring Prime to have a minimally decent experience. Still, when the matchmaking cooperates and a good match falls, CS2 continues to be very addictive. It's that game that you play just once and when you realize it, you've lost track of time.

review:
I've spent 142 days of my life on this game. I'm at peace with that.

3,500 hours buys you specific things: throwing a smoke that lands on the pixel; hearing the first footstep and knowing  which agent skin they paid £15 for; recognising from the opening AK shot whether the player ahead of you has 50 hours or 5,000.

  A few things I now believe:

  - The best CS players aren't the fastest, they're the calmest. Anyone can flick. Almost nobody can hold an angle for
  45 seconds without flinching.
  - Every player has 60ms ping until they die, at which point they had 200ms.
  - "Drop me" is spoken in this game more than any voice line Valve has ever written.
  - A good teammate is the rarest cosmetic in the game.

  Counter-Strike is craft. Most of the time it doesn't love you back. Then it does, for one round, and that pays the
  bill for the next six months.

  Recommended. Obviously.

Almost nobody can hold an angle for 45 seconds without flinching. - Every player has 60ms ping until they die, at which point they had 200ms. - "Drop me" is spoken in this game more than any voice line Valve has ever written. - A good teammate is the rarest cosmetic in the game. Counter-Strike is craft. Most of the time it doesn't love you back. Then it does, for one round, and that pays the bill for the next six months. Recommended. Obviously.

review: В школьные годы и после, в 2005-2008 годах в компьютерных клубах играли с друзьями в КС 1.6 С тех пор не играл ни в КС Го, ни во что-то другое. В 2025 году собрав себе игровой комп решил начать играть в КС2. Поначалу было интересно, я люблю рашит и благодаря этому от неожиданности противников делаю килы и оказываюсь в первой половине игроков по результатам. Но потом разочаровало обилия читеров в играх обычного режима, не понимаю зачем в обычном бесплатном режиме использовать читы? Из-за этого решил купить Прайм статус. После покупки, нужно добраться до 10-го уровня и сделать 100% чтобы открылось Премьер режим, каждую неделю нужно повышать ранги чтобы получить Дропы и ранги лучше повышать аккурат в среду, если раньше повысить это не засчитают для Дропа. Цена Прайм статуса даже с самыми дешевыми дропами окупиться за 2 месяца максимум. Но Прайм статус и игры в Премьер режиме не решают проблему читеров. Античит почти не работает, с повышением ранга попадаются всё больше читеров и софтеров которые со своими ботами фармят кейсы на нескольких аккаунтах, пока он играет за одного игрока его бот стоит АФК и эту проблему Валв не решают никак. В целом лучше не покупать Прайм статус а время от времени поигрывать в обычным режиме, чем купить и стараться окупить игру, играя постоянно с читерами не получая удоволствие

review_english: During my school years and after, in 2005-2008, in computer clubs we played CS 1.6 with friends. Since then I have not played CS Go or anything else. In 2025, I assembled a gaming computer and decided to start playing CS2. At first it was interesting, I love rushing and thanks to this, I surprise my opponents with kills and find myself in the first half of the players in terms of results. But then I was disappointed by the abundance of cheaters in regular mode games; I don’t understand why use cheats in regular free mode? Because of this, I decided to buy Prime status. After the purchase, you need to get to level 10 and make 100 to open the Premier mode, every week you need to increase your ranks to get Drops and it is better to increase your ranks exactly on Wednesday, if you raise it earlier it will not count for the Drop. The price of Prime status, even with the cheapest drops, will pay off in 2 months maximum. But Prime status and games in Premier mode do not solve the problem of cheaters. Anti-cheat almost doesn’t work, as your rank increases, you come across more and more cheaters and softers who, with their bots, farm cases on several accounts, while he plays for one player, his bot costs AFK and Valv doesn’t solve this problem in any way. In general, it is better not to buy Prime status and play in normal mode from time to time, than to buy and try to recoup the game, constantly playing with cheaters without getting any pleasure

review:
Counter-Strike used to feel like the king of competitive shooters, but now it often feels outdated and frustrating. Matches are filled with cheaters, toxic players, and inconsistent matchmaking that can ruin the experience even before the first round ends. The gameplay is still skill-based, but instead of being rewarding, it can feel repetitive and unforgiving for anyone who doesn’t grind constantly.

Despite updates from Valve Corporation, many long-standing problems remain unresolved. Server issues, questionable hit registration, and an overreliance on expensive skins make the game feel more focused on cosmetics than improving the actual player experience. Counter-Strike still has moments of brilliance, but it no longer feels fresh or welcoming compared to modern competitive games.

review_english: Counter-Strike used to feel like the king of competitive shooters, but now it often feels outdated and frustrating. Matches are filled with cheaters, toxic players, and inconsistent matchmaking that can ruin the experience even before the first round ends. The gameplay is still skill-based, but instead of being rewarding, it can feel repetitive and unforgiving for anyone who doesn t grind constantly. Despite updates from Valve Corporation, many long-standing problems remain unresolved. Server issues, questionable hit registration, and an overreliance on expensive skins make the game feel more focused on cosmetics than improving the actual player experience. Counter-Strike still has moments of brilliance, but it no longer feels fresh or welcoming compared to modern competitive games.

review: 一款长盛不衰的高竞技性射击游戏，很公平，操作方便上手简单，想要精通却很难，内容深度不高但手部和脑部乘区高。可惜国区竞技环境太差，外挂太多太猖獗。官匹简单上手之后就建议转战平台了，虽然平台环境也不好，总比官匹休闲全是片姐和透视、脚本，竞技外挂约等于没有监管强点。游戏皮肤存在市场，可以自由交易，但最近因为各种更新水温跌宕起伏，信任几近崩塌，非要买建议就买买喜欢的在能力范围内的，做好亏的准备。有优先可以每周打打箱子什么的，有点微薄的收益。就游戏国区环境而言，如果不抱着非得赢的心去玩还是很不错的，和朋友一起组排体验会比单排好太多，是很值得深玩的游戏。但如果抱着强烈的胜负欲就会玩的很难受很憋屈，毕竟有无耻嘴硬却难以揭穿的作弊者破坏体验，而各种ELO机制又相当恶心人。可惜现在时间不多，没时间也没精力磨练自己的水准了。抛开市场和社区的乱象，谁不想和朋友好好娱乐一把，无所谓输赢。它简单，不代表它简陋，尽管诸多缺点，仍然无法掩盖它是一款好的、成功的游戏的事实。说真的，我在这里只能算是个新手，但我愿意推荐它。

CS2 Review Comment (English):
CS2 is a masterpiece held together by duct tape and prayers. The anti-cheat is a joke—cheaters run rampant while legit players get banned for existing. The hitboxes are so broken you'll swear your bullets teleported to another dimension, and the spray patterns? Completely different from what you practiced for years. Valve keeps tweaking stuff nobody asked for while ignoring the actual game-breaking bugs. It's competitive FPS roulette at this point. Still addictive though, which says more about us than the game. 10/10 emotional damage.

review: Counter-Strike 2 is still one of the best competitive FPS games out there. The gunplay feels sharp, the maps look cleaner than ever, and every round feels like it actually matters. It keeps the classic Counter-Strike formula that made the series legendary while giving it a fresh visual upgrade and smoother presentation. Whether you’re grinding Premier, playing with friends, practicing aim, or just trying to hit clean clips, CS2 has that addictive “one more game” feeling. It can be intense, frustrating, and sweaty at times, but that’s also what makes winning feel so rewarding. If you enjoy tactical shooters with real skill expression, teamwork, and clutch moments, CS2 is absolutely worth playing.

review: Counter-Strike 2 is a technically impressive evolution of CS:GO, featuring brilliant structural overhauls to classic maps and responsive shooting mechanics. However, the current player experience suffers from systemic issues. Valve’s lack of aggressive anti-cheat enforcement allows boosted cheaters to ruin Premier lobbies. Combined with a toxic casual matchmaking culture where team griefing is common, the game is epic when it works, but incredibly frustrating when it doesn't.

- Skins make you better at the game.

review:
Pontos Positivos: A mecânica de tiro está mais refinada e a nova física das smokes (fumaças volumétricas) melhorou muito a estratégia. O Modo Premier e o cenário profissional continuam em alta.
Pontos Negativos: O jogo é mais pesado para rodar, muitos jogadores reclamam da otimização e da falta de sensação de "jogo polido", e o modo competitivo sofre com trapaceiros em ranks mais altos.
review_clean: Pontos Positivos A mecânica de tiro está mais refinada e a nova física das smokes (fumaças volumétricas) melhorou muito a estratégia. O Modo Premier e o cenário profissional continuam em alta. Pontos Negativos O jogo é mais pesado para rodar, muitos jogadores reclamam da otimização e da falta de sensação de "jogo polido", e o modo competitivo sofre com trapaceiros em ranks mais altos.
review_english: Positive Points The shooting mechanics are more refined and the new physics of smokes (volumetric smokes) has greatly improved the strategy. Premier Mode and the professional scene continue to be on the rise. Negative Points The game is heavier to run, many players complain about the optimization and the lack of a "polished game" feel, and the competitive mode suffers from cheaters at higher ranks.

review: This game feels less like Counter-Strike and more like Cheater Strike. Too many matches leave me wondering whether I got outplayed or matched against someone using cheats. The anti-cheat system feels frustratingly weak at times, and it really hurts the experience. On top of that, I’ve run into toxic behavior in the community that makes matches even less enjoyable. The core idea of the game is great, but the overall experience can become frustrating fast.

review: 赛场喧嚣散去，Vitality众人没有急着离场庆祝。法国Apex带着队员，径直走向隔壁TYLOO的训练室。推开门，DANK1NG正和队友整理着装备，还未从刚刚的对决中完全抽离。没有胜负的傲慢，没有赛区的隔阂。Apex的目光径直落在DANK1NG身上，脚步停下，率先伸出了手，他的眼神里没有丝毫轻视，满是对他极致瞄准与绝对冷静的敬佩。身后的TOP1Zywoo与其他队员也跟着点头致意，认可这位来自东方的狙击手。DANK1NG起身，伸手与他们相握。没有多余寒暄，只有高手之间心照不宣的尊重。这是胜者对勇者的致敬，是顶尖赛场里最纯粹的惺惺相惜。一边是如日中天的世界顶级战队，一边是奋力亮剑的中国队伍；一边是运筹帷幄的传奇队长，一边是敢与神明较量的亚一狙。这一次赛后的主动握手，没有惊天动地的声势，却诠释了电竞最动人的内核——胜负之外，尊重永存；强者相逢，不问输赢。它没有赛场对决的热血沸腾，却在每一个热爱CS的人心里，留下了最温暖的印记。

review_english: The hustle and bustle of the stadium dissipated, and everyone in Vitality did not rush to leave the field to celebrate. French Apex led the players and walked straight to the training room of TYLOO next door. They opened the door and DANK1NG was arranging equipment with his teammates. They had not yet completely separated from the previous duel. There was no arrogance about winning or losing, and no barriers between competition areas. Apex's eyes fell directly on DANK1NG. He stopped and took the lead in extending his hand. There was no contempt in his eyes, full of admiration for his extreme aim and absolute calmness. TOP1 Zywoo and other team members behind him also nodded and acknowledged the sniper from the East. DANK1NG stood up and reached out to shake hands with them. There was no unnecessary greeting, only the tacit respect between the masters. This is the winner's tribute to the brave. It is the purest sympathy in the top arena. On one side are the world's top teams in full swing, on the other side is the Chinese team struggling to show off their swords, and on the other side is the legendary captain who is strategizing. On one side is the Yayi sniper who dares to compete with the gods. This post-game handshake did not have earth-shattering momentum, but it interpreted the most touching core of e-sports. Beyond victory and defeat, respect lasts forever. When the strong meet, regardless of winning or losing, it did not have the excitement of a match on the field, but it left the warmest mark in the hearts of everyone who loves CS.

review:
Контр-Страйк 2 — это не просто игра, а настоящее испытание для нервов и выдержки. Каждый матч превращается в череду мучительных моментов, когда сердце замирает в ожидании исхода раунда. Здесь терзания и переживания становятся постоянными спутниками: ты то на грани победы, то на краю поражения.

Графика и атмосфера игры погружают в состояние душевного напряжения, когда каждый шаг может стать последним. Но именно эти испытания делают победу такой сладкой. Ты чувствуешь, как преодолеваешь внутренние муки, учишься справляться с неудачами и радоваться даже самым маленьким успехам.

Контр-Страйк 2 — это история о том, как через борьбу с собой и эмоциональные качели рождается настоящий азарт и командный дух. Эта игра не щадит, но именно поэтому она так цепляет и остаётся в памяти навсегда.

review_english: Counter-Strike 2 is not just a game, but a real test of nerves and endurance. Each match turns into a series of agonizing moments when the heart skips a beat waiting for the outcome of the round. Here, torment and worries become constant companions; you are either on the verge of victory or on the verge of defeat. The graphics and atmosphere of the game immerse you in a state of mental tension, when every step could be your last. But it is these challenges that make victory so sweet. You feel how you overcome internal torment, learn to cope with failures and enjoy even the smallest successes. Counter-Strike 2 is a story about how true passion and team spirit are born through struggle with oneself and emotional swings. This game does not spare, but that is why it is so catchy and remains in the memory forever.
